VAL-BUGFIX-001.evidence: 6 tests in channel_create_leak.rs + 5 tests in channel_create_pte_leak.rs all pass. Tests exercise: (1) first map_channel_page fails after both handles inserted — both handles cleaned up, (2) second map_channel_page fails — both handles cleaned up, (3) slots reusable after cleanup, (4) success path unaffected, (5) PTE rollback on second map failure (fixed version unmaps first page), (6) PTE leak confirmed in buggy model. Kernel syscall.rs lines 248-327: sys_channel_create tracks va_a from first map_channel_page, calls unmap_channel_page(va_a) on second map failure, then closes both handles. Fix matches test model exactly.

VAL-BUGFIX-002.evidence: 4 tests in sched_context_create_leak.rs all pass. Tests exercise: (1) handle table full — context freed (ref_count decremented to 0, slot None), (2) context ID reusable after cleanup, (3) success path unaffected, (4) multiple failures don't accumulate leaked contexts. Kernel syscall.rs lines 906-921: sys_scheduling_context_create calls scheduler::release_scheduling_context(ctx_id) in the map_err closure when handle insert fails. Fix matches test model exactly.
VAL-BUGFIX-002.details: Tests: test_sched_context_create_leak_context_freed_on_full_table, test_sched_context_create_leak_id_reusable_after_cleanup, test_sched_context_create_success_path_unaffected, test_sched_context_create_leak_multiple_failures_dont_accumulate. All 4 pass (3 matched filter, 1 filtered out as it doesn't match the 'leak' pattern but passes in full suite).

VAL-BUGFIX-003.evidence: 7 tests in handle_send_rollback.rs all pass. Tests exercise: (1) second map fails — buggy version leaks 1 page, (2) second map fails — fixed version cleans up (0 leaked pages), (3) handle insert fails — buggy version leaks 2 pages, (4) handle insert fails — fixed version cleans up (0 leaked pages), (5) success path unaffected (both pages mapped, handle inserted), (6) non-channel handle unaffected, (7) target-started returns error immediately. Kernel syscall.rs lines 534-634: sys_handle_send Phase 2 tracks va_a and va_b, unmaps on second map failure (unmap va_a) and on handle insert failure (unmap both va_a and va_b). Fix matches test model exactly.

summary: All 4 assertions pass. 965 tests pass with 0 failures (baseline was 943, 22 new bug fix tests added). Kernel fixes reviewed: sys_channel_create properly rolls back both handles and first PTE on map failure; sys_scheduling_context_create releases context on handle table full; sys_handle_send unmaps channel SHM pages on map or handle insert failure. cargo build --release succeeds cleanly.
